evals.name: spec-website-app
evals.prompt: Spec out the website app at apps/website. Use the editor app's specs at docs/editor/specs/ as a template — same numbered structure, layered on top of the global specs at docs/specs/. The website is an 11ty static site with a marketing hero, three value-prop cards, and features/privacy/terms pages. Write the spec to docs/website/specs/. Make sure cross-links from the per-app spec back to docs/specs/architecture-principles.md and docs/specs/development-guidelines.md are correct.
evals.expected_output: A new directory docs/website/specs/ with numbered markdown files (00-overview.md and a sensible set of detail pages for an 11ty marketing site), a canonical-types.schema.json sidecar, and updates to docs/README.md to index the new spec set. Per-app architecture and development pages open with 'Read first' pointers to the global specs. No 'MVP' framing anywhere in the body. Every file has the Status/Date/Owner header and the closing Assumptions/Decisions/Open questions block.

evals.name: promote-package-spec-to-global
evals.prompt: We have a section in docs/editor/specs/05-design-system.md that talks about the @interrupt/ui package's component contract — how shadcn-svelte components are vendored, the per-component subpath exports, the Storybook discipline, and the iconography rules. Most of that is not editor-specific; it applies to every consumer of @interrupt/ui. Promote the cross-cutting parts to a new global spec at docs/specs/ui-package.md, and rewrite docs/editor/specs/05-design-system.md to be editor-specific (component composition choices, plugin/theme wiring) with a 'Read first' pointer to the new global spec.
evals.expected_output: A new docs/specs/ui-package.md scoped Repo-wide that covers the component contract, subpath exports, Storybook conventions, and iconography rules, with the standard header and closing block. The editor spec at docs/editor/specs/05-design-system.md is rewritten to be a thin per-app file opening with 'Read first: docs/specs/ui-package.md' and only covering editor-specific composition (cross-cutting content removed from it). docs/README.md is updated to index the new global spec. No content is duplicated between the two files.

evals.name: spec-from-scratch-no-template
evals.prompt: Create a spec for a hypothetical new app at apps/journal — a daily-journal app that uses @interrupt/editor for writing, a date-keyed IndexedDB store for entries, and a calendar view to navigate between days. There's no code yet for this app, but we want to spec out the design first so we can implement against it. Write the spec to docs/journal/specs/ following the conventions in docs/specs/ and matching the shape of docs/editor/specs/. Be honest in the spec: since the code doesn't exist yet, the body should describe the *intended* design, but anything ambiguous goes in Open questions. (Acknowledge the divergence — the code doesn't yet exist. The spec is the target the code will be built to match.)
evals.expected_output: A new directory docs/journal/specs/ with numbered markdown files describing the intended journal app — overview, domain model (with at least Entry and CalendarView entities), routes/shell, persistence, and references to global architecture/dev guidelines via 'Read first' pointers. Plus a canonical-types.schema.json. The spec acknowledges in its overview or open-questions section that the code doesn't yet exist and the spec is acting as a target rather than a description of branch reality. No 'MVP' framing.
